title: Server-Driven UI
objectives: Explain what SDUI ships from the server
Design a widget schema and renderer
Weigh SDUI's power vs its risks

sections.markdown: Server-Driven UI (SDUI) means the server sends a description of a screen (JSON) — a tree of typed components with props and actions — that the client renders with pre-built widgets. UI changes without a new app release.

rows: A/B, promos, layout tweaks with no release
Security review of actions is critical

sections.markdown: Big consumer apps (Airbnb Epoxy/BloxUI, Instagram, Zomato) use SDUI for the home feed and promo modules so growth teams ship UI daily. The client is a strict renderer: unknown components are ignored or degraded, never executed as code.
Letting the server ship logic — arbitrary conditionals, scripting, or 'run this expression' payloads. That's an app-store violation risk (native code from a server) and a security hole.
Version your schema, whitelist actions, and reject unknown component types. Keep a fallback native screen for critical flows so SDUI never becomes a single point of failure.

flashcards.back: The server sends a UI description (JSON tree of components); the client renders it with pre-built widgets.
Ship UI changes / A/B tests / personalization without new app releases.

flashcards.front: What must the client NOT do with SDUI?
flashcards.back: Execute arbitrary server-sent code — only render whitelisted components/actions.

quiz.q: SDUI moves which decision to the server?
quiz.explain: Server describes the screen; client renders it.

quiz.q: A key rule for safe SDUI is…
quiz.explain: Only known-safe components render; unknown ones degrade.
High-churn UI benefits most; core critical flows stay native.

challenges.text: Define a JSON schema for Text/Column/Button and render it.
Add actions (navigate, api_call) with a whitelist and safe defaults.
Add schema versioning + graceful degradation for unknown components.
Introduce A/B routing where the server chooses layout variants for a segment.

interviewQuestions: What is SDUI and what does it ship?
When would you use SDUI vs a native screen?
How do you keep SDUI safe?
How do you handle schema versioning?
